text:
Once upon an autumn day, in the quaint little village of Maplewood, there lived a timid goose named Gerald. Gerald lived in a nest he helped build, perched on an oak tree. Despite his timidity, Gerald found joy in the rustling leaves and the songs of birds. One particularly windy morning, Gerald noticed something strange – a shimmer in the air.

Curious, Gerald strutted down the forest path. What he saw next sent waves of alarm through him. A peculiar fog had formed, moving steadily. Gerald tried to fly away quickly, but his wings felt suddenly heavy. The fog touched his feathers, whispering secrets only it seemed to understand.

With each step, Gerald felt weaker. Thoughts of danger flooded his mind. He recalled tales of fog monsters from his youth. Desperate, he flapped his wings furiously until something caught them – a glint of sunlight! It revealed not a monster, but a tiny, gleaming fairy.

"Greetings," the fairy gigg
